name: How do I choose a practical layout for my new kitchen?
headline: How do I choose a practical layout for my new kitchen?

mainEntity.name: How do I choose a practical layout for my new kitchen?
mainEntity.text: How do I choose a practical layout for my new kitchen?

acceptedAnswer.text: Even if you’re not a designer, you can make good layout choices by focusing on **workflow and safety** in your kitchen remodel. Start with the classic “work triangle”: the path between the sink, stove, and refrigerator. You want these three points close enough for easy movement but not so crowded that people bump into each other. Avoid putting these key pieces too far apart or right next to doors where traffic is heavy. Think about where tasks happen. Food prep needs counter space near the sink and fridge. Cooking requires space beside the stove for pans and ingredients. Cleaning needs room around the sink and dishwasher so doors can open without hitting walls or island corners. It’s also important to plan clear walking paths. Try not to place islands or tall cabinets where they block views or make the room feel cramped. In smaller kitchens, a peninsula or open shelving might work better than a full island. Common mistakes include squeezing in oversized islands, placing the fridge where it blocks a doorway, or forgetting landing space next to appliances. Before you commit, tape outlines of cabinets and islands on the floor and “test drive” the space: walk your daily cooking routine and see what feels off. If you’re unsure, asking a local designer or contractor for a quick layout review can help you avoid costly rework later.
